audit_result.proof_trace: After `_multiCall(calls)` executes at L109, there is no mechanism to reset token allowances granted to addresses other than `s_auction`. The only post-multiCall approval action (L111) sets `assetOut` allowance for `msg.sender` (the auction), leaving any approvals to arbitrary spenders set during `_multiCall` persistent. This allows `AUCTION_BIDDER_ROLE` to include `token.approve(attacker, MAX_UINT)` calls in the solution, creating a persistent allowance that bypasses the `DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE` restriction on `withdraw()`.
audit_result.attack_scenario: `AUCTION_BIDDER_ROLE` includes `Call(token, abi.encodeWithSelector(IERC20.approve.selector, attacker, type(uint256).max))` in the `solution` passed to `bid()`; after `auctionCallback` completes the approval persists, allowing the attacker to call `token.transferFrom(AuctionBidder, attacker, balance)` and drain the contract's holdings in a follow-up transaction.

PROP-01b-partial-inv-004.text: Token approvals set during _multiCall callback must not persist for arbitrary spenders beyond intended scope.
PROP-01b-partial-inv-004.assertion: forall _multiCall(calls): post(_multiCall) => allowance(this, spender) == 0 for all spender != s_auction
